title: Honoring Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., by celebrating diversity, promoting tolerance, and condemning hate.
plain_summary: This bill honors Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. on what would have been his 96th birthday. The House of Representatives wants to celebrate his teachings about treating all people fairly and with respect. The bill speaks out against discrimination and harassment of minority groups. It encourages everyone to follow Dr. King's example of working for justice and peace.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
policy_area: Civil Rights and Liberties, Minority Issues

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution honors Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., by celebrating diversity, promoting tolerance, and condemning hate.

sections.title: Background Information
sections.text: The nation will celebrate the ninety-sixth anniversary of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.'s birth on Monday, January twentieth, twenty twenty-five. Dr. King taught that having people of many different backgrounds is a blessing that makes our country stronger. He fought, marched, and preached for the basic rights that belong to all people, especially Black Americans. One of these important rights is that every person should be able to vote and have their voice heard in our democracy where people elect their leaders. It is a great virtue to accept and respect other people's differences in race, ethnicity, gender, culture, religion, sexual orientation, age, or disability. Having respectful conversations in a free democratic country is what America truly promises to be. Dr. King once wrote in his famous letter from Birmingham Jail in nineteen sixty-three that whatever affects one person directly affects everyone else indirectly.

sections.title: What the House of Representatives Resolves
sections.text: The House of Representatives celebrates the ninety-sixth anniversary of Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.'s birthday on January twentieth, twenty twenty-five. This day should remind us that hate and hateful actions based on people's differences have no place in the United States, in honor of his life and teachings. The House affirms Dr. King's words that anyone who lives inside the United States can never be considered an outsider anywhere within the country's borders. The House honors the life, legacy, works, and heroism of Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. The House condemns any form of harassment, discrimination, or prejudice targeted at any minority ethnic group, including the Black community, Indigenous people, the Jewish community, the Asian-American and Pacific Islander community, the Muslim community, and the Hispanic and Latino communities. The House condemns any form of harassment, discrimination, or prejudice based on a person's gender, including the trans community and LGBTQ+ individuals. The House abhors the violence and hatred that led to the untimely death of Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. The House calls on all people across the nation and the world to uphold Dr. King's values and teachings of justice, equality, peace, advocacy, and tolerance.
